data.title: Adagene announces $200M mixed shelf offering
data.description:

  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: FAA considers 'broad' reductions to flights into Newark International Airport - report
data.description:
On the heels of two air traffic control outages at Newark Liberty Airport in a little over a week, the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration is considering a measure to cut back on the number of flights in and out of the busy airport.

On April 28 and again on Friday, air traffic controllers for the airport experienced a blackout of their radar systems that lasted for a crucial 90 seconds, during which time, all incoming flights were unable to communicate with traffic controllers. Fortunately, neither incident resulted in accidents or injuries.

According to Bloomberg, the FAA is considering asking airlines to voluntarily reduce the number of flights handled by the airport in an effort to relieve the strain on the main air traffic control center for Newark. The control center handling flights for Newark was moved to Philadelphia from Long Island to ease the strain on the Long Island employees managing flights for LaGuardia and JFK. 

However, the antiquated system in Philadelphia coupled with staffing shortages created an imminent threat that was exacerbated by increased traffic into Newark and construction of a new runway.

Addressing the situation to airline and aircraft manufacturing executives, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy unveiled a plan on Thursday to replace outdated radar and communication systems, and increase the number of air traffic controllers across the U.S. at a cost of more than $30B.

“The system we have here is not worth saving, it’s too old,” Duffy told industry executives yesterday.

The investment is being applauded by airline executives who have complained for years about the failures within the U.S. air traffic control system, specifically United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) CEO Scott Kirby who has voluntarily reduced the number of flights the carrier flies into Newark to address safety and scheduling concerns.

The carrier has already cut the number of flights into Newark three times, currently 30% less than prior to the pandemic, Bloomberg said.

In a note to employees viewed by CNBC, Kirby has been urging the government for years to use its authority to limit the number of flights to what Newark can handle.

“Past failure to make those changes had led to the circumstances that United, and most importantly, our customers now face.”

data.title: Biglari Holdings reports Q1 results
data.description: • Biglari Holdings press release (NYSE:BH): Q1 net loss of $33.28M

data.title: NI Holdings reports Q1 results
data.description:
• NI Holdings press release (NASDAQ:NODK): Q1 Direct written premiums of $67.7 million compared to $83.0 million. This reduction was driven by Non-Standard Auto (-58.8%) due to strategic decisions to exit Nevada and significantly reduce written premium in the Chicago market to improve profitability, partially offset by Home and Farm (7.1%) driven by higher new business in North Dakota as well as rate and insured value increases.
  • Combined ratio of 94.4% driven by strong performance in Home and Farm and Private Passenger Auto.
  • Net investment income increased 3.0% to $2.8 million, driven by relatively consistent yields on a higher average invested asset base.
  • Basic earnings per share of $0.31 compared to basic earnings per share of $0.33

data.title: PriceSmart appoints Gualberto Hernandez as CFO
data.description:
• PriceSmart (NASDAQ:PSMT) announced on Friday the appointment of Gualberto Hernandez as executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective June 1, 2025.
  • PriceSmart's current Executive Vice President and CFO Michael McCleary resigned by mutual agreement with the company, but will continue to serve as an Executive Vice President of the company through September 30, 2025.
  • Hernandez has served as Vice President Finance & Strategy for Latin America for The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. since January 2016.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sable Offshore reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Sable Offshore press release (NYSE:SOC): Q1 net loss of $109.5 million, primarily attributable to production restart related operating expenses, non-cash interest expense, and a non-cash change in fair value of warrant liabilities.
  • Concluded the quarter with outstanding debt of $854.6 million, inclusive of paid-in-kind interest, additional principal incurred from the debt amendment, and debt issuance costs.
  • Ended the quarter with cash and cash equivalents balance of $189 million, exclusive of restricted cash balance of $35.5 million.

data.title: Sibanye Stillwater hikes Finland lithium project cost estimate to $880M
data.description:
Sibanye Stillwater (NYSE:SBSW) said Friday it has raised the estimated cost of its Keliber lithium project in Finland to €783M (~$880M) from €667M previously, due primarily to additional regulatory requirements and changes to the scope of the venture.

Sibanye (NYSE:SBSW) owns 79.8% the Keliber project, which is expected to produce at least 15K metric tons/year of battery-grade lithium for 16 years starting in 2026.

Sibanye (SBSW) said construction of the project is "well advanced" and the refinery would be operational in Q1 2026, adding that a total of €508M had been spent on the project by the end of March, the company added.

The company said last August that it had obtained €500M of debt financing, partially funded by the European Investment Bank, to bring the Keliber project into production in 2026.

data.title: SA Asks: Which chip stocks are most attractive right now?
data.description:
Which semiconductor stocks are most attractive right now?

Seeking Alpha analysts Brett Ashcroft Green and KM Capital gave us their picks.

Brett Ashcroft Green: Chip stocks remain undervalued as a group, with iShares Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ:SOXX) and VanEck Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ:SMH) both in bear market territory. 

I, personally, like SOXX (NASDAQ:SOXX) as it has less concentration at the top than SMH (NASDAQ:SMH). As for individual names, I’m sticking with the two that have the largest moats and expected forward growth rates, Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and ASML (NASDAQ:ASML). 

Equipment names as a whole look like the best overall play in chips right now, with Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) and KLA (NASDAQ:KLAC) also looking like good values, despite their lower growth rates compared to ASML (NASDAQ:ASML). Their machines all deal with different stages of the semiconductor production process, and all will get business in the U.S. semi fab build outs that are in the pipeline.

KM Capital: While I recently upgraded my rating for AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) due to the extremely attractive valuation, I still think that over the long-haul they will struggle to keep up with Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) pace of innovation. 

Nvidia (NVDA) remains my top chip stock pick due to its unparalleled pricing power backed by aggressive innovation, which ensures best-in-class profitability and FCF generating capacity. With much higher scale and profitability compared to AMD (NASDAQ:AMD), Nvidia (NVDA) also has many more resources to invest in growth. In addition, Nvidia (NVDA) is not just a chip company but represents a diversified AI ecosystem and boasts a circa 90% GPU market share. With all these unrivaled positives being mentioned, I think that NVDA (NVDA) is an absolute steal at this share price given the FY2028 estimated forward P/E ratio of below 20.  

I'd also pick Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) because Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) does not directly compete with NVDA (NVDA) but is also a vital wide-moat player providing a rich variety of semiconductor products for AI data centers beyond GPUs or CPUs.

  • Top Semiconductor Stocks
